name: D+Q AD-risk pilot provides feasibility-oriented cognition and mobility signals
summary: The 2025 single-arm pilot in older adults at risk for Alzheimer's disease reported 12 intervention completers, no related serious events, an imprecise overall MoCA increase, an exploratory MoCA increase in the lowest-baseline subgroup, and an inverse TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation.
statement: After 12 weeks of intermittent D+Q, the AD-risk pilot reported mean MoCA +1.0 point overall, +2.0 points in the lowest-baseline-MoCA subgroup, TNF-alpha -3.0%, and an inverse TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation, all requiring cautious interpretation because the study lacked a control group.
population_or_model: Older adults at risk for Alzheimer's disease
time_horizon: Baseline to completion of 12-week intermittent D+Q course
caveats: Small single-arm design limits causal inference and precision.
Mobility endpoints are not yet separately extracted from the full article.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 40010154 abstract METHODS, FINDINGS, and INTERPRETATION
provenance.note: Abstract supports D+Q dose schedule, completion count, MoCA estimates, TNF-alpha estimate, and TNF-alpha/MoCA correlation.

name: D+Q bone RCT needs primary-null and subgroup-signal separation
summary: The postmenopausal women bone trial has a null primary 20-week CTx result for D+Q versus control, short-term P1NP secondary signals at 2 and 4 weeks, no 20-week P1NP difference, and exploratory subgroup signals tied to baseline senescent-cell burden.
statement: The phase 2 randomized bone trial should be represented as mixed human evidence: D+Q did not reduce the primary 20-week bone resorption marker CTx in the overall group, but secondary P1NP measures favored D+Q at 2 and 4 weeks and exploratory subgroup findings require separate labeling.
time_horizon: Phase 2 trial follow-up period for bone metabolism outcomes
caveats: Primary endpoint, exploratory subgroup, and biomarker outcomes must be extracted separately before any synthesis claim.
Exploratory high-p16 T3 subgroup records should remain separated from overall randomized endpoints.
Publication adverse-event table blanks are not encoded as zeros because the table reports events above a reporting threshold.
Bone metabolism evidence should not be generalized to global aging outcomes.

provenance.locator: PubMed abstract key results for PMID 38956196
provenance.note: Abstract reports primary CTx, secondary P1NP, serious adverse event, and exploratory high-senescent-cell-burden findings.
ClinicalTrials.gov posted results provide group medians, ranges, p-values, and adverse-event aggregate counts.
Full-text extraction separates overall null primary endpoint context, exploratory high-p16 subgroup signals, and publication adverse-event term table.

name: D+Q diabetic kidney disease pilot reported decreased senescent-cell biomarkers
summary: The preliminary diabetic kidney disease trial reported that 9 participants received dasatinib 100 mg plus quercetin 1000 mg for 3 days and had lower senescent-cell and SASP-related biomarker signals 11 days after treatment completion.
statement: A small open-label human trial reported reduced adipose and skin senescent-cell markers and circulating SASP factors after a 3-day D+Q course in diabetic kidney disease.
time_horizon: Short-course intermittent treatment with post-treatment tissue and circulating biomarker assessment
caveats: This is preliminary biomarker evidence from a small open-label trial and should not be treated as clinical efficacy.
Registry metadata checked in 2026 indicates the associated ClinicalTrials.gov record has no posted results and remains operationally relevant for follow-up.

provenance.locator: PubMed PMID 31542391 abstract METHODS, FINDINGS, and INTERPRETATION
provenance.note: Abstract supports sample size, dose, timing, and directional biomarker-decrease claim; exact biomarker table values remain unextracted.

name: D+Q IPF randomized pilot primarily supports feasibility and tolerability boundaries
summary: The idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis randomized pilot provides human safety and feasibility evidence, but it is not a definitive efficacy trial.
statement: The randomized IPF pilot is useful for feasibility, tolerability, and endpoint-boundary evidence, but small sample size and pilot design prevent strong efficacy inference.
time_horizon: Pilot intervention and follow-up period reported in the phase 1 study
caveats: Treat as safety and feasibility evidence unless a later synthesis verifies a powered efficacy endpoint.
Functional outcomes should remain separate from aging-healthspan claims.

provenance.note: Triage-level generated record pending source-located extraction.

name: Intermittent D+Q improved physical function and post-treatment survival in old mice
summary: The 2018 aged-mouse paper reported functional improvements and increased post-treatment survival after intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin.
statement: Intermittent dasatinib plus quercetin was associated with reduced senescent-cell burden, improved physical function, and increased post-treatment survival in old mice.
population_or_model: Old mice treated intermittently with dasatinib plus quercetin
time_horizon: Post-treatment survival and physical-function follow-up in aged mouse cohorts
caveats: Animal lifespan and healthspan signals do not establish human aging efficacy.
The finding should not be pooled with human functional outcomes without a separate translational synthesis boundary.

provenance.note: Triage-level generated record pending source-located extraction.

name: D+Q discovery work showed senescent-cell clearance and preclinical functional signals
summary: The Achilles heel discovery paper anchors the senolytics mechanism by showing that senescent-cell pro-survival pathways can be targeted and that dasatinib plus quercetin reduced senescent-cell burden and improved selected functional outcomes in mouse models.
statement: The study identified senescent-cell anti-apoptotic vulnerabilities and reported that dasatinib plus quercetin reduced senescent-cell burden in aged, irradiated, and progeroid mice, with short-term cardiac and vascular improvements and longer exercise-capacity benefit after limb irradiation.
population_or_model: Senescent human and mouse cell assays plus aged, irradiated, and progeroid mouse models
time_horizon: Single-dose short-term functional measures and periodic dosing in progeroid mice
quantitative_note: The abstract reports improved cardiac function and carotid vascular reactivity 5 days after a single dose in old mice and improved exercise capacity for at least 7 months after irradiation in one limb.
caveats: This is preclinical and mechanistic evidence, not human aging efficacy.
The study covers several models and endpoints, so translation depends on which senescent-cell populations are targeted in each tissue.
Financial conflicts and senolytic patents were disclosed.

provenance.note: Triage-level generated record pending source-located extraction.

name: SToMP-AD D+Q phase 2 trial is active without posted results
summary: The SToMP-AD phase 2 dasatinib plus quercetin registry record is active-not-recruiting and had no posted ClinicalTrials.gov results at the checked registry update.
statement: The registry record establishes an active phase 2 D+Q Alzheimer's disease trial-watch item but contributes no result-bearing efficacy evidence until results are posted or published.
population_or_model: Adults with Alzheimer's disease enrolled in SToMP-AD
caveats: Registry-only evidence must not be counted as a treatment effect.
Needs surveillance for posted results or linked publication.

name: Fisetin vascular function trial is active without posted results
summary: The fisetin vascular function trial in older adults is active-not-recruiting and had no posted ClinicalTrials.gov results at the checked registry update.
statement: The registry record establishes an active fisetin trial-watch item but contributes no result-bearing efficacy evidence until posted or published results are available.
population_or_model: Older adults in a phase 1/2 fisetin vascular function trial
caveats: Registry-only evidence must not be counted as a positive or negative treatment effect.
Needs surveillance for posted results or linked publication.

name: Senolytic translation review supports mechanism and boundary framing, not pooled efficacy
summary: The 2020 translation review is useful for intervention definitions, candidate classes, and safety boundary conditions, but it should not be counted as independent efficacy evidence.
statement: The review supports taxonomy and translation framing for senolytics but should be excluded from quantitative efficacy synthesis as non-independent narrative evidence.
population_or_model: Review of senolytic discovery and translation literature
caveats: Do not use as a primary result record for effect estimation.
Useful for defining senolytic mechanism, safety considerations, and unresolved trial needs.

provenance.note: Triage-level generated record pending source-located extraction.
